name: Foundation Reset
description: Shift mental model from calling models to building the systems that train models. Understand the raw training loop -- forward pass, loss, backward pass, optimizer step -- at the code level, not the API level.
gate_check_text: Can I modify the training loop (change optimizer, add gradient clipping, swap scheduler) without docs? Y/N

name: PyTorch Internals & GPU Fundamentals
description: Think like a systems engineer about training. Understand memory, compute, and where bottlenecks live.
gate_check_text: Can I read a torch.profiler trace and explain what the GPU was doing at each point? Y/N

name: Distributed Training
description: This is the core competency the role demands. Make training work across multiple GPUs and nodes.
gate_check_text:
Can I shard a model that OOMs on 1 GPU across 4 GPUs, train it, checkpoint it, and resume? Y/N
Can I explain DDP vs FSDP vs DeepSpeed ZeRO tradeoffs? Y/N

name: RLHF & Alignment Algorithms
description: Understand the full post-training pipeline that produces models like Claude. This is the domain-specific knowledge the role requires.
gate_check_text:
Can I run the full SFT -> RM -> PPO pipeline end-to-end? Y/N
Can I explain PPO vs DPO vs GRPO tradeoffs clearly? Y/N
Have I done this distributed across multiple GPUs? Y/N

name: Systems Engineering & Performance
description: Bridge from ML practitioner who can train models to systems engineer who makes training infrastructure fast, reliable, and usable.
gate_check_text:
Can I profile end-to-end and distinguish GIL vs GPU vs data loading bottlenecks? Y/N
Do I have working infra utilities (launcher, checkpointer, metrics)? Y/N

name: Agentic AI & Training Systems
description: Combine your strong agentic AI background with training systems knowledge. This is your differentiator -- most training infra people don't understand agents, and most agent builders don't understand training.
gate_check_text:
Can I explain how tool-use gets trained INTO models (not just prompted)? Y/N
Have I trained a model to use tools via RL? Y/N

name: Portfolio & Open Source
description: Build visible proof of your new skills. Code speaks louder than bullet points. Clean GitHub repo, OSS contributions, and technical blog posts.
gate_check_text:
Does my GitHub repo demonstrate training infrastructure competency? Y/N
Do I have at least 1 merged OSS PR on a training framework? Y/N
Are at least 2 blog posts published? Y/N
